title: nvm_write: INVALID id server behavior undocumented
description: nvm_write() doc comment explains what happens when id \!= NvmId::INVALID (delete first, then add) but is silent on what happens when id IS NvmId::INVALID. The code skips deletion and passes 0 directly to wh_Client_NvmAddObject. Whether the server auto-assigns an ID, rejects the call, or treats 0 as a valid ID is not described anywhere in the Rust API surface. A caller using nvm_write(NvmId::INVALID, ...) as 'create a new object' has no way to know if that is supported or what ID is assigned. Doc comment should state: 'If id is NvmId::INVALID the existing-object deletion is skipped and id 0 is passed to the server; server behavior for id 0 is [expected behavior here].'
